display.purpose: Landing hero headlines only, serif, fluid-sized.

title.purpose: Every CardTitle in the system — dashboard stats, dialogs, report sections.

body.purpose: Default UI text, descriptions, interactive controls.

label.purpose: Mono, uppercase, tracked micro-labels — the studio's signage system: section eyebrows, live-state indicators, timestamps.

label-sm.purpose: Same signage voice one step down — speaker labels inside a transcript entry, tile name tags.

shadows.name: panel-highlight
shadows.purpose: Resting state for every card/panel (studio-panel) — a top-lit surface, not elevation.

shadows.name: signal-glow
shadows.purpose: The system's one emphasis tool (studio-glow) — primary CTA, active tile, the single most important card on screen. Used sparingly.

shadows.name: tile-frame
shadows.purpose: Separates a video-call tile from the panel behind it. The one shadow in the system that isn't purely a glow.

motion.name: ease-studio
motion.purpose: Default easing for reveals, tile swaps, and gauge fills — a confident ease-out, never a bounce.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one high-emphasis action on a screen — Start interview, Sign in, Submit answer.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.625rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid transparent; background: oklch(0.75 0.14 60); color: oklch(0.18 0.03 50); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: all 150ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(0.75 0.14 60) 80%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.75 0.14 60); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 50%); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary/dismissive action next to a primary one.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.625rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.94 0.015 75 / 10%); background: transparent; color: oklch(0.94 0.015 75); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.27 0.022 55); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.75 0.14 60); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 50%); }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: End/cancel a session, delete a record — a confirmed, hard-stop action.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2rem; padding: 0 0.625rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: none; background: oklch(0.62 0.16 30 / 10%); color: oklch(0.62 0.16 30); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: oklch(0.62 0.16 30 / 20%); }

components.name: Secondary Badge
components.description: Metadata tag — interview type, difficulty, count.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 1.25rem; padding: 0 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: oklch(0.27 0.022 55); color: oklch(0.94 0.015 75); font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Panel Card
components.description: The base instrument-panel surface — flat, hairline-bordered, no shadow at rest.
components.html:
Your progress

12 interviews practiced this month.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; background: oklch(0.225 0.02 55); box-shadow: 0 1px 0 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.04) inset; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.94 0.015 75); margin: 0; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.68 0.03 65); margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field — email, password, code entry.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 2rem; width: 100%; padding: 0 0.625rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.94 0.015 75 / 16%); background: transparent; color: oklch(0.94 0.015 75); font-size: 0.875rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.68 0.03 65); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.75 0.14 60); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 50%); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; background: oklch(0.27 0.022 55 / 50%); }

components.name: Studio Tile
components.description: Signature component — the interviewer/candidate video-call tile. Active-speaker state swaps a hairline border for an amber border + signal glow.
components.css: .ds-tile { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 4 / 3; width: 20rem; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 0.375rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.94 0.015 75 / 10%); background: oklch(0.225 0.02 55); box-shadow: 0 14px 34px -22px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); transition: border-color 200ms; } .ds-tile-active { border-color: oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 55%); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px oklch(0.75 0.14 60); } .ds-tile-label { position: absolute; bottom: 0.5rem; left: 0.5rem; z-index: 10; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.225 0.02 55 / 80%); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.94 0.015 75); } .ds-tile-dot { width: 0.375rem; height: 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.75 0.14 60); animation: ds-tally-pulse 1.8s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-tally-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 55%); } 50% { opacity: 0.55; box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px oklch(0.75 0.14 60 / 0%); } }

narrative.overview:
interview.ai is styled as a broadcast/recording studio, not a SaaS dashboard. The candidate is stepping into a booth for a live take: a tally light indicates who's speaking, a signal chain of instrument panels carries the interview's stages, and a stage spotlight drifts quietly across the page. This is a deliberate departure from the generic AI-product template — no purple-to-blue gradient hero, no chat-bubble message UI with circular avatars, no Inter-as-default-sans, no rounded-everything card grid repeated as the answer to every layout question. Warm graphite stands in for stark near-black; one committed amber signal accent stands in for a multi-color gradient; an editorial serif carries headings instead of the safe sans-everywhere look every AI tool defaults to.

The system is built dark-first and ships dark-only today (the light theme is fully specified in code but not yet wired to a toggle) — the studio is dim, the panels are lit, and the one warm accent reads as a signal in the dark, not decoration. Depth comes from light, not elevation: surfaces stay flat and bordered at rest, and a soft ambient glow — never a drop shadow — marks the one thing on a screen that matters most.
keyCharacteristics: One committed accent (Tally Amber) used sparingly and consistently — never as decoration
Editorial serif for headings, a clean grotesque for body, monospace for every label/timer/score — three fonts, three distinct jobs
Flat panels with a subtle inset top highlight at rest; a soft amber glow is the only elevation signal
Mono-cased, letter-spaced micro-labels function as the studio's signage system — restrained, not decorative
Ambient atmosphere (grain, vignette, drifting spotlight) runs globally at very low opacity, fully suppressed under reduced motion

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Tally Amber is the only accent in the system. A second saturated color anywhere outside Alarm's destructive-only role is drift.

rules.name: The Warm Hairline Rule
rules.body: Never introduce a plain gray border. Every border and divider derives from the Ink color at low opacity.

rules.name: The Three-Job Rule
rules.body: Serif is for headings and titles only. Sans is for body and interactive text only. Mono is for labels, timers, and numbers only.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: If an element needs to read as 'above' the page, use a border and the Panel background, not a drop shadow.

dos: Do use Tally Amber as the only accent color in the system.
Do keep every neutral tinted toward the same warm hue family as the amber accent.
Do use the flat-panel-plus-glow elevation model, glow reserved for the single most important element in view.
Do reserve the mono uppercase tracked label style for the studio's signage system, consistently.
Do give the interviewer and candidate tiles identical chrome so the active-speaker swap is the only visual difference.
donts: Don't use a purple-to-blue gradient hero, chat-bubble UI, or any other piece of the generic AI-product template.
Don't default to Inter, Space Grotesk, or another safe sans as a display font.
Don't use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe — a recognized AI-generated-UI tell.
Don't use a drop shadow to imply elevation anywhere in the system.
Don't nest a bordered card-like box inside another Card — use a divider inside one panel instead.
Don't introduce a second saturated accent color outside Alarm's destructive-only role, even for data visualization.
Don't apply the signal glow to more than one or two elements on a single screen.
